serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of getuk ubi is one piece weighing approximately 80 g, commonly consumed as a morning snack or teatime kueh.

nutrient_source.title: Getuk Ubi — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Getuk Ubi (mashed tapioca cake with grated coconut, steamed). Confidence 0.95 as reported in source record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for getuk ubi. GI of 94 is based on cassava (tapioca) as the primary starch base, referenced from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) and the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cassava/tapioca. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 94 × (35.8 × 0.80) / 100 ≈ 27.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 160 mg/100 g and high saturated fat (~5.9 g/100 g) from grated coconut
hypertension.guidance: Limit portion to one small piece (≤80 g) and avoid adding salt during preparation; the borderline sodium content combined with coconut-derived saturated fat can contribute to arterial stiffness and elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI cassava starch (GI ~94) with 35.8 g carbohydrate/100 g causing significant postprandial glucose spikes
type2_diabetes.guidance: Restrict to a very small portion (≤40 g) and pair with a protein or low-GI food to blunt the glycaemic response; the high-GI cassava base causes rapid blood glucose elevation that is only partially moderated by the 5.9 g dietary fibre from coconut.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 5.9 g/100 g from grated coconut, which is excluded from the MUFA/PUFA beneficial-fat carve-out
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Consume occasionally and in small portions (≤80 g); the coconut-derived saturated fat raises LDL cholesterol and the high-GI starch promotes triglyceride elevation, both of which are independent cardiovascular risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~5.9 g/100 g from coconut (excluded from MUFA/PUFA carve-out) and estimated sugar ~12 g/100 g promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Limit or avoid during active RA flares; coconut-derived saturated fat activates TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways, and the estimated sugar content (~12 g/100 g) further amplifies systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Getuk Ubi is a traditional Indonesian and Malay steamed kueh (cake) made from mashed tapioca (cassava) mixed with freshly grated coconut, sugar, and a pinch of salt. The cassava is peeled, boiled or steamed until soft, then mashed and combined with grated coconut before being shaped into small rectangular or cylindrical pieces. The texture is dense, slightly sticky, and moist, with a mildly sweet flavour and a subtle coconut fragrance. It is commonly coloured with natural pandan or food colouring and served as a breakfast item or afternoon snack (kueh). Nutritionally, getuk ubi provides 213 kcal per 100 g, with 35.8 g carbohydrate, 6.9 g fat (predominantly saturated fat from coconut, approximately 5.9 g/100 g), 1.7 g protein, and 5.9 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 160 mg/100 g. The cassava base carries a very high glycaemic index (~94), making this snack unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes unless consumed in very small portions paired with protein. The saturated fat from grated coconut raises concern for cardiovascular disease and hypertension management. Despite its high-GI profile, the dietary fibre from coconut provides some satiety benefit. Getuk ubi is gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal, making it accessible to a wide range of dietary preferences in Singapore's multicultural food landscape.
common_user_queries: How many calories in getuk ubi?
What is the GI of cassava cake?
Is getuk ubi suitable for hypertension?
What are the ingredients in getuk ubi?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. GI is an analogous estimate based on cassava from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (peer-reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from known coconut composition as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; this is the only field requiring estimation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for getuk ubi are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry as of 2026-04-16, due to limited published data for this traditional preparation. The HPB Foodo database was consulted and confirmed that these fields are not available for this entry. saturated_fat_g (5.9 g/100 g) is estimated from the known fatty acid composition of grated coconut (approximately 85% of coconut fat is saturated) applied to the reported total fat of 6.9 g/100 g; this estimation is consistent with USDA FoodData Central coconut data (NDB 12104). sugar_g (12.0 g/100 g) is estimated from typical getuk ubi formulations as HPB does not separately report sugar; this value is used in health context reasoning. cholesterol_mg set to 0.0: getuk ubi is a plant-based product containing no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero is appropriate. GI of 94 is an analogous estimate based on cassava/tapioca from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283).
